articles.title: Traders Boost BOE and ECB Rate-Hike Bets After Oil Price Surge
articles.summary: Traders boosted wagers on faster Bank of England and European Central Bank interest-rate hikes after surging oil prices reignited inflation fears.

articles.title: Asian Oil Buyers Look to US Crude as Iran War Intensifies
articles.summary: The escalation of hostilities between Washington and Tehran and the virtual standstill in observable traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is prompting Asian refiners to look at buying more US oil.

articles.title: China Widens Australian Canola Trade to Include Private Crushers
articles.summary: China will allow its private oilseed crushers to buy Australian canola, as the two countries move to normalize a trade that’s largely been halted since the start of the decade.

articles.title: Korean Won Climbs to Two-Month High on Hynix ADR Inflows
articles.summary: The South Korean won advanced to its strongest level since May, supported by dollar inflows tied to SK Hynix Inc.’s American depositary receipts, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Iran Sneaks Out Tankers Via Hormuz as Trump Amps Up Threats
articles.summary: Iran has reverted to its well-used tactic of going dark to move oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz in recent days, even before the US said it would reimpose a blockade of the country’s ports.

articles.title: Rathbones Cut Gilt Exposure in Case Burnham ‘Does a Truss’
articles.summary: Rathbones Asset Management has reduced its holdings of UK government bonds to avoid a potential selloff if Andy Burnham boosts spending and raises borrowing.

articles.title: Africa’s $219 Billion Fund Suspends CEO as Internal Rift Deepens
articles.summary: The board of Africa’s largest state-run fund suspended Chief Executive Officer Patrick Dlamini in a late-night move that highlighted the deepening feud at the $219 billion asset manager.
